wire_delay_model.links_note: 8x8 grid on 20mm x 20mm die, 2.5 mm tile pitch. 7nm delta=162.0 ps/mm, clock 5 GHz (period 200.00 ps). cardinal link 2.5 mm = 3 cycle(s); diagonal link 3.536 mm = 3 cycle(s). Generator: /tmp/hexa-arch-rfc001-measurement/runs/gen_topologies_v2.py.

router_cost.port_cost_basis: d6/d4 = 1.516x -- comb SKY130 yosys+abc measured (d4=61763 um^2, d6=93608 um^2), comb HANDOFF_TO_HEXA_ARCH.md §4

verdict.rationale: single-topology record; F1/F2 verdict requires the pair-verdict aggregator over this + a degree-paired mate at identical (traffic, node, clock, wire_delay_model)

wire_delay_source.node_basis: SMART 22nm delta=90 ps/mm extrapolated to 7nm via Naeemi/Georgia Tech public copper-RC scaling (+21.8% 22->11nm, +48% 11->7nm; 90*1.218*1.48=162 ps/mm) -- EXTRAPOLATION, not a direct 7nm characterization

leighton_source.edition: Leighton 1984 + Leighton 1992 1.4; Dally & Towles PPIN 2004 3.2-3.3 mesh closed forms

scope_caveats: single (5 GHz, 7nm, d6_brick placement) point under tornado -- one cell of the rfc_001 9 sweep, not a standalone regime claim
router_cost.port_*_norm is an analytic degree-scaling estimate (T1A 2 / Dally-Towles port-count), NOT measured silicon area
routing = min (DOR-class shortest-path); adaptive routing may shift saturation throughput

7nm delta=162 ps/mm is an EXTRAPOLATION from SMART 22nm via Naeemi/Georgia Tech copper-RC scaling -- no direct public 7nm wire-delay characterization was found in the survey; treat as sensitivity bound, not ground truth
brick/offset-row hex placement: all 6 neighbours at true pitch a (no sqrt(2) diagonal penalty); hop-diameter 11 -- a strictly better hex placement than axial-on-square
